---
title: "Balancing Class Attribute"
output: html_document
date: "2024-11-10"
---


# Opening File 

```{r}
file_path <- "C:/Users/jivko/Documents/Data Analytics, Big Data, and Predictive Analytics/Personal Project/Credit_Score_Multiclass/Credit_score_cleaned_data.csv"
```
```{r}
credit_score <- read.csv(file_path, header = TRUE)
```

# Printing head of dataset

```{r}
head(credit_score)
```


# Seeking count of class attribute

```{r}
table(credit_score$Credit_Score)
```


# Balancing class attribute - Undersampling "Poor" and "Standard" to 17828

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# Define the target count based on the minority class "Good"
target_count <- sum(credit_score$Credit_Score == "Good")  # 17,828

# Undersample Poor and Standard classes to match the target count
undersampled_poor <- credit_score[credit_score$Credit_Score == "Poor", ][sample(nrow(credit_score[credit_score$Credit_Score == "Poor", ]), target_count), ]
undersampled_standard <- credit_score[credit_score$Credit_Score == "Standard", ][sample(nrow(credit_score[credit_score$Credit_Score == "Standard", ]), target_count), ]

# Combine the balanced dataset
balanced_data <- bind_rows(
  credit_score[credit_score$Credit_Score == "Good", ],  # Keep all "Good" instances
  undersampled_poor,
  undersampled_standard
)

# Check the new class distribution
new_class_dist <- table(balanced_data$Credit_Score)
print("New Class Distribution after Balancing:")
print(new_class_dist)
```

# Checking Strucutre of Dataset

```{r}
str(credit_score)
```

# Exporting to CSV File 

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# Define the target count based on the minority class "Good"
target_count <- sum(credit_score$Credit_Score == "Good")  # 17,828

# Undersample Poor and Standard classes to match the target count
undersampled_poor <- credit_score[credit_score$Credit_Score == "Poor", ][sample(nrow(credit_score[credit_score$Credit_Score == "Poor", ]), target_count), ]
undersampled_standard <- credit_score[credit_score$Credit_Score == "Standard", ][sample(nrow(credit_score[credit_score$Credit_Score == "Standard", ]), target_count), ]

# Combine the balanced dataset
balanced_data <- bind_rows(
  credit_score[credit_score$Credit_Score == "Good", ],  # Keep all "Good" instances
  undersampled_poor,
  undersampled_standard
)

# Check the new class distribution
new_class_dist <- table(balanced_data$Credit_Score)
print("New Class Distribution after Balancing:")
print(new_class_dist)

# Export the balanced dataset to a CSV file
output_path <- "C:/Users/jivko/Documents/Data Analytics, Big Data, and Predictive Analytics/Personal Project/Credit_Score_Multiclass/balanced_data_creditscore.csv"
write.csv(balanced_data, file = output_path, row.names = FALSE)

print(paste("Balanced dataset exported to:", output_path))
```
